When the day dawned, `Abdullah said, "The Prophet never offered any prayer at this hour except this prayer at this time and at this place and on this day." `Abdullah added, "These two prayers are shifted from their actual times -- the Maghrib prayer (is offered) when the people reached Al-Muzdalifa and the Fajr (morning) prayer at the early dawn." `Abdullah added, "I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) doing that."
citations.urdu: عبدالرحمن بن یزید سے روایت ہے کہ عبداللہ بن مسعود رضی اللہ عنہ نے حج کیا اور ہم عشاء کے وقت یا اس کے قریب مزدلفہ پہنچے۔ انہوں نے ایک شخص کو حکم دیا کہ اذان اور اقامت کہے، پھر مغرب کی نماز پڑھی اور اس کے بعد دو رکعت پڑھیں، پھر شام کا کھانا منگوا کر کھایا، پھر (دوبارہ) اذان اور اقامت کہلوائی اور عشاء کی دو رکعت پڑھیں۔ جب فجر طلوع ہوئی تو عبداللہ نے کہا: نبی کریم صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم اس وقت، اسی جگہ اور اسی دن کے علاوہ یہ نماز کبھی نہیں پڑھتے تھے۔ عبداللہ نے مزید کہا: یہ دو نمازیں اپنے وقت سے ہٹائی گئی ہیں، مغرب کی نماز جب لوگ مزدلفہ پہنچیں اور فجر کی نماز جب فجر طلوع ہو، اور کہا: میں نے نبی کریم صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم کو ایسا کرتے دیکھا ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Abdur Rahman bin Yazid se riwayat hai keh Hazrat Abdullah bin Masood (RA) ne Hajj kiya aur hum Isha ke waqt ya uske qareeb Muzdalifah pahunchay. Unhon ne ek shakhs ko hukum diya ke azan aur iqamat kahe, phir Maghrib ki namaz parhi aur uske baad do rakat parhi, phir shaam ka khana mangwa kar khaya, phir dobara azan aur iqamat kahlwai aur Isha ki do rakat parhi. Jab Fajr tulu hui to Abdullah ne kaha: Nabi Kareem (SAW) is waqt, isi jagah aur isi din ke ilawa yeh namaz kabhi nahi parhte thay. Abdullah ne mazeed kaha: yeh do namazein apne waqt se hatai gayi hain, Maghrib ki namaz jab log Muzdalifah pahunchen aur Fajr ki namaz jab Fajr tulu ho, aur kaha: maine Nabi Kareem (SAW) ko aisa karte dekha hai.
citations.relevance: Confirms combining Maghrib and Isha at Muzdalifah with a light supper in between (not optional prayers), and highlights that Fajr at Muzdalifah is also prayed very early — supporting the key point about the Fajr prayer being observed there with special timing.

citations.arabic: حَتَّى أَتَى الْمُزْدَلِفَةَ فَصَلَّى بِهَا الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءَ بِأَذَانٍ وَاحِدٍ وَإِقَامَتَيْنِ وَلَمْ يُسَبِّحْ بَيْنَهُمَا شَيْئًا ثُمَّ اضْطَجَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم حَتَّى طَلَعَ الْفَجْرُ وَصَلَّى الْفَجْرَ حِينَ تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ الصُّبْحُ بِأَذَانٍ وَإِقَامَةٍ ثُمَّ رَكِبَ الْقَصْوَاءَ حَتَّى أَتَى الْمَشْعَرَ الْحَرَامَ فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ فَدَعَاهُ وَكَبَّرَهُ وَهَلَّلَهُ وَوَحَّدَهُ فَلَمْ يَزَلْ وَاقِفًا حَتَّى أَسْفَرَ جِدًّا
citations.english: [From the long narration of Jabir bin `Abdullah describing the Prophet's Hajj]: ...until he (the Prophet) came to Muzdalifa. There he led the evening (Maghrib) and 'Isha' prayers with one Adhan and two Iqamas and did not glorify (Allah) in between them (i.e. he did not observe supererogatory rak'ahs between Maghrib and 'Isha' prayers). The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) then lay down till dawn and offered the dawn (Fajr) prayer with an Adhan and Iqama when the morning light was clear. He again mounted al-Qaswa, and when he came to al-Mash'ar al-Haram, he faced towards Qibla, supplicated Him, Glorified Him, and pronounced His Uniqueness (La ilaha illa Allah) and Oneness, and kept standing till the daylight was very clear.
citations.urdu: (حضرت جابر بن عبداللہ رضی اللہ عنہما کی طویل روایت سے، جس میں نبی کریم صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم کے حج کی تفصیل بیان کی گئی ہے): یہاں تک کہ آپ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم مزدلفہ پہنچے۔ وہاں ایک اذان اور دو اقامتوں کے ساتھ مغرب اور عشاء کی نماز پڑھائی اور ان کے درمیان کوئی نفل نماز نہیں پڑھی۔ پھر رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم فجر تک لیٹے رہے اور جب صبح واضح ہو گئی تو اذان اور اقامت کے ساتھ فجر کی نماز پڑھی۔ پھر قصویٰ (اونٹنی) پر سوار ہوئے یہاں تک کہ مشعرِ حرام پہنچے، قبلہ رخ ہوئے، دعا کی، تکبیر کہی، تہلیل کی اور اللہ کی وحدانیت بیان کی، اور برابر کھڑے رہے یہاں تک کہ روشنی بہت واضح ہو گئی۔
citations.romanUrdu: (Hazrat Jabir bin Abdullah (RA) ki lambi riwayat se, jismein Nabi Kareem (SAW) ke Hajj ki tafseel bayan ki gayi hai): Yahan tak keh Aap (SAW) Muzdalifah pahunchay. Wahan ek azan aur do iqamaton ke saath Maghrib aur Isha ki namaz parhai aur inke darmiyan koi nafl namaz nahi parhi. Phir Rasoolullah (SAW) Fajr tak lait rahay aur jab subah wazeh ho gayi to azan aur iqamat ke saath Fajr ki namaz parhi. Phir Qaswa (untni) par sawar huay yahan tak keh Mash'ar-ul-Haram pahunchay, Qibla rukh huay, dua ki, takbeer kahi, tahleel ki aur Allah ki wahdaniyat bayan ki, aur barabar khare rahay yahan tak keh roshni bohat wazeh ho gayi.
citations.grading: Sahih (Sahih Muslim, Book 15, Hadith 159 — the detailed Hajj narration of Jabir bin `Abdullah, via Ja'far al-Sadiq from his father Muhammad al-Baqir)
citations.relevance: This is the Prophet's own eyewitness-transmitted account of his practice at Muzdalifah during the Farewell Hajj: he stayed overnight, prayed Maghrib and Isha combined, lay down until dawn, prayed Fajr at first light, then proceeded to al-Mash'ar al-Haram and stood facing the Qiblah in supplication and remembrance until it was very bright before departing for Mina. It is the foundational and most authoritative hadith supporting the ruling that pilgrims should remain at Muzdalifah until after Fajr and engage in standing dhikr/dua at al-Mash'ar al-Haram, directly supporting the claimed Muzdalifah ruling.

rulingSummary: A pilgrim must spend the nights of the Tashreeq days in Mina, though the Prophet (SAW) permitted Al-Abbas to stay in Mecca instead due to his duty of supplying water to pilgrims, and one may depart Mina either on the 12th of Dhul Hijjah (Nafr Awwal) or wait until the 13th (Nafr Thani) without sin. Prayers offered during the stay at Mina are to be shortened (Qasr), as practiced by the Prophet (SAW), Abu Bakr, and Umar (RA).
keyPoints: It is permissible to depart Mina on the 12th of Dhul Hijjah (Nafr Awwal); one who delays and stays until the 13th (Nafr Thani) also commits no sin, provided taqwa is observed (Quran 2:203).

The Fard prayers are to be prayed as Qasr (shortened, two rak'at) while at Mina during Hajj, as the Prophet (SAW), Abu Bakr, and Umar (RA) all did; Uthman (RA) also did so in the early part of his caliphate before later praying the full prayer (Sahih al-Bukhari 1082).

Staying overnight (mabit) in Mina during the nights of the Tashreeq days is an established obligation, from which one may be excused only for a valid reason of necessity or service, as shown by the Prophet (SAW) granting Al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib permission to spend those nights in Mecca on account of his duty of supplying water (siqayah) to the pilgrims (Sahih Muslim 1315a).

citations.arabic: وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ فِىٓ أَيَّامٍۢ مَّعْدُودَٰتٍۢ ۚ فَمَن تَعَجَّلَ فِى يَوْمَيْنِ فَلَآ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَمَن تَأَخَّرَ فَلَآ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ لِمَنِ ٱتَّقَىٰ ۗ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّكُمْ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ
citations.english: And remember Allah during ˹these˺ appointed days. Whoever departs swiftly on the second day is not sinful, neither are those who stay behind ˹till the third—seeking additional reward˺, so long as they are mindful ˹of their Lord˺. And be mindful of Allah, and know that to Him you will ˹all˺ be gathered.
citations.urdu: اور اللہ کو گنتی کے چند دنوں میں یاد کرو، پھر جو شخص دو دن میں جلدی کرے (یعنی بارہویں کو منیٰ سے چلا جائے) اس پر کوئی گناہ نہیں، اور جو پیچھے رہے (یعنی تیرہویں تک ٹھہرے) اس پر بھی کوئی گناہ نہیں، بشرطیکہ وہ تقویٰ اختیار کرے۔ اور اللہ سے ڈرتے رہو اور جان لو کہ تم سب اسی کی طرف جمع کیے جاؤ گے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Aur ALLAH ko in gine chune dinon (ayyam e tashreeq) mein yaad karo. Phir jo koi do din mein jaldi karay (yani 12 Zul Hijjah ko Mina se chala jaye) us par koi gunah nahi, aur jo peechay reh jaye (yani 13 tak ruke) us par bhi koi gunah nahi, bashart yeh ke woh taqwa ikhtiyar kare. Aur ALLAH se darte raho aur jaan lo ke tum sab usi ki taraf jama kiye jao ge.
citations.relevance: This ayah directly establishes that a pilgrim may leave Mina either on the 12th (Nafr Awwal) or wait until the 13th (Nafr Thani) without sin, which is the primary Quranic basis for the ruling on when it is permissible/more virtuous to depart Mina.

citations.arabic: صَلَّيْتُ مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِمِنًى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، وَأَبِي بَكْرٍ، وَعُمَرَ، وَمَعَ عُثْمَانَ صَدْرًا مِنْ إِمَارَتِهِ ثُمَّ أَتَمَّهَا
citations.english: Narrated Abdullah bin Umar: I offered the prayer with the Prophet, Abu Bakr and 'Umar at Mina and it was of two rak'at. 'Uthman in the early days of his caliphate did the same, but later on he started praying the full prayer.
citations.urdu: عبداللہ بن عمر رضی اللہ عنہما سے روایت ہے: میں نے نبی کریم صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم کے ساتھ منیٰ میں دو رکعت نماز پڑھی، اور ابوبکر اور عمر رضی اللہ عنہما کے ساتھ بھی، اور عثمان رضی اللہ عنہ کے ساتھ ان کی خلافت کے ابتدائی دور میں بھی (دو رکعت پڑھی)، پھر بعد میں انہوں نے پوری نماز پڑھنا شروع کر دی۔
citations.romanUrdu: Hazrat Abdullah bin Umar (RA) se riwayat hai: Maine Nabi Kareem (SAW) ke sath Mina mein do rakat namaz parhi, aur Abu Bakr aur Umar (RA) ke sath bhi, aur Usman (RA) ke sath unki khilafat ke shuru daur mein bhi (do rakat parhi), phir baad mein unhon ne poori namaz parhna shuru kar di.
citations.grading: Sahih (Sahih al-Bukhari, Book of Shortening the Prayers - At-Taqsir, Book 20)
citations.relevance: This is the direct, classical proof-text that the Prophet (SAW), Abu Bakr, and Umar (RA) all prayed the Fard prayers as Qasr (two rak'at) while at Mina during Hajj, and that Uthman (RA) also did so in the early part of his caliphate before later praying the full prayer. It directly answers the pilgrim's question on Qasr vs. full prayer in Mina.

citations.arabic: أَنَّ الْعَبَّاسَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ اسْتَأْذَنَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ يَبِيتَ بِمَكَّةَ لَيَالِيَ مِنًى مِنْ أَجْلِ سِقَايَتِهِ فَأَذِنَ لَهُ
citations.english: Ibn 'Umar reported that al-'Abbas b. 'Abd al-Muttalib sought permission from Allah's Messenger (may peace be upon him) to spend in Mecca the nights (which he was required to spend) at Mina, on account of his office of supplier of water, and he (the Holy Prophet) granted him permission.
citations.urdu: عباس بن عبدالمطلب رضی اللہ عنہ نے رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم سے اجازت مانگی کہ وہ منیٰ کی راتیں مکہ میں گزار سکیں کیونکہ وہ حاجیوں کو پانی پلانے کی خدمت (سقایہ) پر مامور تھے، تو آپ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے انہیں اجازت دے دی۔
citations.romanUrdu: Abbas bin Abdul Muttalib (RA) ne Rasoolullah (SAW) se ijazat mangi ke woh Mina ki raatein Makkah mein guzar saken kyunke woh hajiyon ko pani pilane ki zimmedari (siqayah) par mamoor thay, to Aap (SAW) ne unhe ijazat de di.
citations.grading: Sahih (Sahih Muslim, Book of Pilgrimage - Chapter: It is obligatory to stay overnight in Mina during the nights of the days of At-Tashriq, and the concession allowing those who supply water to leave)
citations.relevance: This hadith is the classical proof-text establishing that staying overnight in Mina during the Tashreeq nights is an obligation from which one is excused only for a valid service-related reason (used by scholars to derive the Wajib ruling on mabit in Mina and its exceptions).

rulingSummary: Umrah is a highly virtuous act of worship that Allah has commanded be completed together with Hajj, and one who begins it must see it through; performing it in Ramadan carries reward equivalent to Hajj, it must be entered into via Ihram from the proper Miqat, and it may be performed on behalf of an elderly or incapacitated parent unable to travel, by analogy to the same ruling for Hajj.
keyPoints: Allah commands believers to "complete the Hajj and Umrah for Allah" (Quran 2:196), which is the primary basis for Umrah's obligatory-in-nature status once undertaken, and this ayah also underlies the ruling on Tamattu' (combining Umrah with Hajj).
Umrah carries immense spiritual reward: performing one Umrah expiates the minor sins committed between it and the next Umrah (Sahih al-Bukhari 1773).

Umrah performed specifically during Ramadan carries a reward equivalent to performing Hajj with the Prophet (peace be upon him) (Sahih al-Bukhari 1863), showing Umrah is not restricted to the Hajj season and is especially encouraged in Ramadan.

Pilgrims intending Umrah must enter Ihram from the designated Miqat for their direction of travel (e.g., Dhul-Hulaifa, Al-Juhfa, Qarn al-Manazil, Yalamlam), or from the point they start if living within the Miqat boundaries, and Makkans assume Ihram from Makkah itself (Sahih al-Bukhari 1524); knowingly crossing the Miqat without Ihram while intending Umrah requires a return to it or payment of a dam.

A person may perform the pilgrimage on behalf of an elderly or physically incapacitated parent who cannot sit firm on a mount or travel; though the foundational hadith (Sahih al-Bukhari 1513) speaks of Hajj, scholars extend the same ruling to Umrah by analogy (qiyas), since the underlying reason - physical incapacity - applies equally to both.

citations.arabic: وَأَتِمُّوا الْحَجَّ وَالْعُمْرَةَ لِلَّهِ ۚ فَإِنْ أُحْصِرْتُمْ فَمَا اسْتَيْسَرَ مِنَ الْهَدْيِ ۖ وَلَا تَحْلِقُوا رُءُوسَكُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ الْهَدْيُ مَحِلَّهُ ۚ ... فَإِذَا أَمِنتُمْ فَمَن تَمَتَّعَ بِالْعُمْرَةِ إِلَى الْحَجِّ فَمَا اسْتَيْسَرَ مِنَ الْهَدْيِ...
citations.english: And complete the ḥajj and ʿumrah for Allāh. But if you are prevented, then [offer] what can be obtained with ease of sacrificial animals. And do not shave your heads until the sacrificial animal has reached its place of slaughter... And when you are secure, then whoever performs ʿumrah [during the ḥajj months] followed by ḥajj [offers] what can be obtained with ease of sacrificial animals...
citations.urdu: اور حج اور عمرہ اللہ کے لیے مکمل کرو۔ پس اگر تمہیں روک دیا جائے تو جو قربانی میسر ہو (پیش کرو)۔ اور اپنے سر نہ مونڈو جب تک قربانی اپنی جگہ نہ پہنچ جائے... اور جب تم امن میں ہو جاؤ تو جو شخص حج تک عمرہ سے فائدہ اٹھائے وہ جو قربانی میسر ہو پیش کرے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Aur Hajj aur Umrah Allah ke liye poora karo. Agar rok diye jao to jo qurbani mumkin ho wo pesh karo, aur apne sar na mundwao jab tak qurbani apni jagah na pohnch jaye... Aur jab aman mil jaye to jo shakhs Hajj tak Umrah se faida uthaye wo jo qurbani mumkin ho pesh kare.
citations.relevance: This is the primary Quranic command establishing Umrah alongside Hajj as an act to be completed for Allah, and it explicitly links Umrah to Hajj (Tamattu'), which is the basis scholars use to derive its obligatory (Wajib/Fard) status.

citations.arabic: الْعُمْرَةُ إِلَى الْعُمْرَةِ كَفَّارَةٌ لِمَا بَيْنَهُمَا، وَالْحَجُّ الْمَبْرُورُ لَيْسَ لَهُ جَزَاءٌ إِلاَّ الْجَنَّةُ
citations.english: Narrated Abu Huraira: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "(The performance of) 'Umra is an expiation for the sins committed (between it and the previous one). And the reward of Hajj Mabrur (the one accepted by Allah) is nothing except Paradise."
citations.urdu: ابوہریرہ رضی اللہ عنہ سے روایت ہے کہ رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے فرمایا: ایک عمرہ سے دوسرے عمرہ تک درمیان کے (صغیرہ) گناہوں کا کفارہ ہو جاتا ہے، اور حج مبرور کا بدلہ صرف جنت ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Hazrat Abu Huraira (RA) se riwayat hai ke Rasoolullah (SAW) ne farmaya: Ek Umrah se doosre Umrah tak darmiyan ke gunahon ka kaffara ho jata hai, aur Hajj Mabroor ka badla sirf Jannat hai.
citations.grading: Sahih (Authentic) — Sahih al-Bukhari, Book 26 ('Umrah), Chapter 1, Hadith 1 (overall hadith no. 1773)
citations.relevance: Establishes the great spiritual reward/virtue of Umrah — that it expiates minor sins committed between one Umrah and the next, directly relevant to why pilgrims are encouraged to perform it.

citations.arabic: وَقَّتَ لأَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ ذَا الْحُلَيْفَةِ، وَلأَهْلِ الشَّأْمِ الْجُحْفَةَ، وَلأَهْلِ نَجْدٍ قَرْنَ الْمَنَازِلِ، وَلأَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ يَلَمْلَمَ، هُنَّ لَهُنَّ وَلِمَنْ أَتَى عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ غَيْرِهِنَّ، مِمَّنْ أَرَادَ الْحَجَّ وَالْعُمْرَةَ، وَمَنْ كَانَ دُونَ ذَلِكَ فَمِنْ حَيْثُ أَنْشَأَ، حَتَّى أَهْلُ مَكَّةَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ
citations.english: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) made Dhul-Hulaifa as the Miqat for the people of Medina; Al-Juhfa for the people of Sham; Qarn-al-Manazil for the people of Najd; and Yalamlam for the people of Yemen; and these Mawaqit are for the people at those very places, and besides them for those who come through those places with the intention of performing Hajj and 'Umra; and whoever is living within these boundaries can assume Ihram from the place he starts, and the people of Mecca can assume Ihram from Mecca.
citations.urdu: حضرت ابن عباس رضی اللہ عنہما سے روایت ہے کہ رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے اہلِ مدینہ کے لیے ذوالحلیفہ، اہلِ شام کے لیے جحفہ، اہلِ نجد کے لیے قرن المنازل اور اہلِ یمن کے لیے یلملم کو میقات مقرر فرمایا۔ یہ میقات وہاں کے رہنے والوں کے لیے ہیں اور ان لوگوں کے لیے بھی جو حج و عمرہ کے ارادے سے وہاں سے گزریں۔ اور جو ان حدود کے اندر رہتا ہو وہ جہاں سے چاہے احرام باندھ لے، یہاں تک کہ اہلِ مکہ مکہ ہی سے احرام باندھیں۔
citations.romanUrdu: Hazrat Ibn Abbas (RA) se riwayat hai ke Rasoolullah (SAW) ne Madinay walon ke liye Zul-Hulaifa, Shaam walon ke liye Juhfa, Najd walon ke liye Qarn-ul-Manazil aur Yemen walon ke liye Yalamlam ko Miqat muqarrar farmaya. Ye Miqat un jagahon ke rehne walon ke liye hain aur un logon ke liye bhi jo Hajj ya Umrah ki niyat se wahan se guzrein. Aur jo in hadood ke andar rehta ho wo jahan se chahe ehram bandh le, yahan tak ke Makkah walay Makkah se hi ehram bandhein.
citations.relevance: Establishes the Miqat boundaries for Ihram — directly answers questions about people going to Jeddah (which lies within the Miqat) and needing to know from where to enter Ihram for Umrah; crossing the Miqat without Ihram while intending Umrah requires dam unless one returns to it.

citations.arabic: إِنَّ فَرِيضَةَ اللَّهِ عَلَى عِبَادِهِ فِي الْحَجِّ أَدْرَكَتْ أَبِي شَيْخًا كَبِيرًا، لاَ يَثْبُتُ عَلَى الرَّاحِلَةِ، أَفَأَحُجُّ عَنْهُ قَالَ نَعَمْ
citations.english: Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Abbas: Al-Fadl (his brother) was riding behind Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and a woman from the tribe of Khath'am came and Al-Fadl started looking at her (and she started looking at him), and the Prophet (ﷺ) turned Al-Fadl's face to the other side. The woman said, 'O Allah's Messenger! The obligation of Hajj enjoined by Allah on His devotees has become due on my father, and he is old and weak, and he cannot sit firm on the Mount; may I perform Hajj on his behalf?' The Prophet (ﷺ) replied, 'Yes, you may.' That happened during the Hajj-al-Wida (of the Prophet).
citations.urdu: حضرت عبداللہ بن عباس رضی اللہ عنہما سے روایت ہے کہ خثعم قبیلے کی ایک عورت نے عرض کیا: یا رسول اللہ! اللہ نے اپنے بندوں پر حج فرض کیا ہے اور میرے والد بہت بوڑھے ہیں، سواری پر مضبوطی سے نہیں بیٹھ سکتے، کیا میں ان کی طرف سے حج کر سکتی ہوں؟ آپ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے فرمایا: ہاں، کر سکتی ہو۔ یہ واقعہ حجۃ الوداع کے موقع پر پیش آیا۔
citations.romanUrdu: Hazrat Abdullah bin Abbas (RA) se riwayat hai ke Khath'am qabeele ki ek aurat ne arz kiya: Ya Rasoolullah! Allah ne apne bandon par Hajj farz kiya hai aur mere walid bahut boorhe hain, sawari par mazbooti se nahi baith sakte, kya main unki taraf se Hajj kar sakti hoon? Aap (SAW) ne farmaya: Haan, kar sakti ho. Ye waqia Hajjat-ul-Wida ke mauqe par pesh aaya.
citations.relevance: This is the foundational Hajj-specific hadith establishing that a child may perform the pilgrimage on behalf of an elderly/incapacitated parent who cannot physically travel or sit firm on a mount. Although the hadith's literal wording addresses Hajj, scholars (via qiyas/analogy) extend this same ruling to Umrah performed on behalf of an elderly or incapacitated parent, since the underlying legal reasoning (physical incapacity preventing personal performance of the pilgrimage) applies equally. This is a well-established application in Islamic jurisprudence, directly relevant to a pilgrim asking about performing Umrah for an old father.

citations.arabic: فَإِنَّ عُمْرَةً فِي رَمَضَانَ تَقْضِي حَجَّةً مَعِي
citations.english: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: When the Prophet (ﷺ) returned after performing his Hajj, he asked Um Sinan Al-Ansari, "What did forbid you to perform Hajj?" She replied that her husband had two camels, one used for Hajj and the other for irrigating their land. The Prophet (ﷺ) said to her, "Perform 'Umra in the month of Ramadan, (as it is) equivalent to Hajj (or Hajj with me in reward)."
citations.urdu: حضرت ابن عباس رضی اللہ عنہما سے روایت ہے کہ نبی کریم صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے ام سنان انصاریہ سے پوچھا: تمہیں حج سے کس چیز نے روکا؟ انہوں نے بتایا کہ ان کے شوہر کے پاس دو اونٹ تھے، ایک پر حج کیا اور دوسرا زمین کی سینچائی کے لیے تھا۔ آپ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے فرمایا: رمضان میں عمرہ کرو، یہ حج کے برابر (ثواب میں میرے ساتھ حج کرنے کے برابر) ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Hazrat Ibn Abbas (RA) se riwayat hai ke Nabi Kareem (SAW) ne Umme Sinan Ansariya se pucha: Tumhe Hajj se kis cheez ne roka? Unhon ne bataya ke unke shohar ke paas do oont thay, ek par Hajj kiya aur doosra zameen ki sinchai ke liye tha. Aap (SAW) ne farmaya: Ramadan mein Umrah karo, ye Hajj ke barabar (sawab mein mere sath Hajj karne ke barabar) hai.
citations.relevance: Establishes the special virtue of performing Umrah specifically in Ramadan, which carries reward equivalent to Hajj — commonly cited to encourage pilgrims and clarify Umrah is not restricted to Hajj season.

rulingSummary: Visiting Madinah and praying in Masjid an-Nabawi is a mustahabb (highly recommended) act carrying immense reward, but it is not a rite or condition of Hajj/Umrah — those unable to travel there lose nothing, since salawat and salam sent from anywhere in the world still reach the Prophet ﷺ.
keyPoints: Traveling with the specific intention of visiting Masjid an-Nabawi is sanctioned by the Prophet ﷺ, who named it as one of only three mosques worth undertaking a dedicated journey to (Sahih al-Bukhari 1189).

A single prayer in Masjid an-Nabawi is rewarded as equal to 1,000 prayers in any other mosque except Masjid al-Haram, so pilgrims are encouraged to pray as many salah as possible there during their stay (Sahih al-Bukhari 1190; Sahih Muslim 1394a).

Madinah holds a special spiritual status, described by the Prophet ﷺ as a land that purifies its people of bad elements the way a furnace removes impurities from iron — a reminder to bear the hardships of the journey with patience (Sahih al-Bukhari 1871).
Ziyarah of the Prophet's ﷺ grave should follow proper adab: sending salawat and salam upon him, while avoiding turning the grave into a recurring gathering or festival site (Sunan Abi Dawud 2042).

Sending salawat and salam upon the Prophet ﷺ is a Quranic command (Quran 33:56) and is not restricted to being physically present in Madinah - Allah has appointed angels who convey the greetings of the Ummah to him from wherever a person may be, so those unable to perform ziyarah in person are not deprived of this reward (Sunan an-Nasa'i 1282; Sunan Abi Dawud 2042).

The mosques of Madinah (understood by scholars to include Masjid Quba and, on another view, Masjid an-Nabawi itself) are described in the Quran as built upon taqwa from their very first day, underscoring the sanctity of the city's places of worship (Quran 9:108).

citations.arabic: لاَ تُشَدُّ الرِّحَالُ إِلاَّ إِلَى ثَلاَثَةِ مَسَاجِدَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ، وَمَسْجِدِ الرَّسُولِ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَمَسْجِدِ الأَقْصَى
citations.english: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Do not set out on a journey except for three Mosques i.e. Al-Masjid-Al-Haram, the Mosque of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), and the Mosque of Al-Aqsa, (Mosque of Jerusalem)."
citations.urdu: نبی کریم صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے فرمایا: صرف تین مسجدوں کی طرف سفر کے ارادے سے رخت سفر باندھا جائے: مسجدِ حرام، میری اس مسجد (مسجدِ نبوی) اور مسجدِ اقصیٰ۔
citations.romanUrdu: Nabi Kareem SAW ne farmaya: sirf teen masjidon ki taraf jaanay ke liye khaas safar kiya jaye — Masjid-e-Haram, meri ye masjid (Masjid-e-Nabawi), aur Masjid-e-Aqsa.
citations.grading: Muttafaqun Alayhi (agreed upon by Bukhari and Muslim; also in Sahih Muslim 1397). Narrated by Abu Huraira.
citations.relevance: Establishes that traveling specifically to visit Masjid an-Nabawi in Madinah is a recognized, rewarded act sanctioned by the Prophet ﷺ, being one of only three mosques worth a dedicated journey.

citations.arabic: صَلاَةٌ فِي مَسْجِدِي هَذَا خَيْرٌ مِنْ أَلْفِ صَلاَةٍ فِيمَا سِوَاهُ إِلاَّ الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرَامَ
citations.english: Narrated Abu Huraira: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "One prayer in my Mosque is better than one thousand prayers in any other mosque excepting Al-Masjid-Al-Haram."
citations.urdu: ابوہریرہ رضی اللہ عنہ سے روایت ہے کہ رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے فرمایا: میری اس مسجد میں ایک نماز پڑھنا مسجدِ حرام کے سوا کسی اور مسجد کی ایک ہزار نمازوں سے بہتر ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Abu Hurairah RA se riwayat hai keh Rasoolullah SAW ne farmaya: meri is masjid mein aik namaz parhna, Masjid-e-Haram ke ilawa kisi aur masjid ki hazar namazon se behtar hai.
citations.relevance: Directly establishes the immense reward of praying in Masjid an-Nabawi, the primary reason pilgrims are encouraged to maximize their salah there during their stay in Madinah.

citations.arabic: صَلاَةٌ فِي مَسْجِدِي هَذَا أَفْضَلُ مِنْ أَلْفِ صَلاَةٍ فِيمَا سِوَاهُ إِلاَّ الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرَامَ
citations.english: Abu Huraira (Allah be pleased with him) narrated it directly from Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) having said this: A prayer in my mosque is a thousand times more excellent than a prayer in any other mosque, except Masjid al-Haram (Mosque of the Ka'ba).
citations.urdu: ابوہریرہ رضی اللہ عنہ نے براہ راست رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم سے روایت کی کہ آپ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے فرمایا: میری اس مسجد میں ایک نماز، مسجدِ حرام کے سوا کسی اور مسجد کی ایک ہزار نمازوں سے افضل ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Abu Hurairah RA ne seedha Rasoolullah SAW se riwayat ki keh aap SAW ne farmaya: meri is masjid mein aik namaz, Masjid-e-Haram ke ilawa kisi aur masjid ki hazar namazon se afzal hai.
citations.relevance: Establishes the specific, well-known reward multiplier (1000x) for prayer in Masjid an-Nabawi in Madinah relative to other mosques (excepting Masjid al-Haram), and corroborates the parallel narration in Sahih al-Bukhari (e.g. Bukhari 1190), confirming authenticity and wide transmission of this ruling relevant to pilgrims visiting Madinah.

citations.arabic: أُمِرْتُ بِقَرْيَةٍ تَأْكُلُ الْقُرَى يَقُولُونَ يَثْرِبُ، وَهْىَ الْمَدِينَةُ، تَنْفِي النَّاسَ كَمَا يَنْفِي الْكِيرُ خَبَثَ الْحَدِيدِ
citations.english: Narrated Abu Huraira: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "I was ordered to migrate to a town which will swallow (conquer) other towns and is called Yathrib and that is Medina, and it turns out (bad) persons as a furnace removes the impurities of iron."
citations.urdu: ابوہریرہ رضی اللہ عنہ سے روایت ہے کہ رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے فرمایا: مجھے ایک ایسی بستی کی طرف ہجرت کا حکم دیا گیا جو دوسری بستیوں کو نگل جائے گی، اسے یثرب کہتے ہیں اور وہی مدینہ ہے، یہ لوگوں کو اس طرح نکال باہر کرتی ہے جیسے بھٹی لوہے کے میل کچیل کو نکال دیتی ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Abu Hurairah RA se riwayat hai keh Rasoolullah SAW ne farmaya: mujhe aik aisi basti ki taraf hijrat ka hukum diya gaya jo doosri bastiyon ko nigal jayegi, usay Yathrib kehte hain aur wahi Madinah hai, ye logon ko usi tarah nikaal deti hai jaise bhatti lohay ki mail nikaal deti hai.
citations.relevance: Shows the special spiritual quality of Madinah — that it purifies its residents and visitors by expelling impurity/bad elements just as a furnace removes impurities from iron — supporting the point that pilgrims should bear its hardships with patience and good intention.

citations.arabic: لاَ تَجْعَلُوا بُيُوتَكُمْ قُبُورًا وَلاَ تَجْعَلُوا قَبْرِي عِيدًا وَصَلُّوا عَلَىَّ فَإِنَّ صَلاَتَكُمْ تَبْلُغُنِي حَيْثُ كُنْتُمْ
citations.english: Narrated AbuHurayrah: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: Do not make your houses graves, and do not make my grave a place of festivity. But invoke blessings on me, for your blessings reach me wherever you may be.
citations.urdu: ابوہریرہ رضی اللہ عنہ سے روایت ہے کہ نبی کریم صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے فرمایا: اپنے گھروں کو قبرستان نہ بناؤ اور میری قبر کو میلہ گاہ نہ بناؤ، بلکہ مجھ پر درود بھیجتے رہو کیونکہ تمہارا درود مجھ تک پہنچتا ہے، تم جہاں بھی ہو۔
citations.romanUrdu: Abu Hurairah RA se riwayat hai keh Nabi Kareem SAW ne farmaya: apne gharon ko qabristan mat banao aur meri qabar ko mela mat banao, balkeh mujh par durood parhtay raho kyunkeh tumhara durood mujh tak pahunchta hai, tum jahan bhi ho.
citations.grading: Sahih (graded Sahih by Shaykh Al-Albani; found in Sunan Abi Dawud, Kitab al-Manasik wa'l-Hajj, chapter on visiting graves)
citations.relevance: Establishes both that salawat reaches the Prophet (peace be upon him) regardless of physical distance (so travel to Madinah is not required for salawat to reach him) and cautions against turning his grave into a repeated gathering/festival site, guiding the proper limited adab of ziyarah.

citations.arabic: إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَلاَئِكَةً سَيَّاحِينَ فِي الأَرْضِ يُبَلِّغُونِي مِنْ أُمَّتِي السَّلاَمَ
citations.english: It was narrated that Abdullah (ibn Mas'ud) said: "The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Allah has angels who travel around on Earth conveying to me the Salams of my Ummah.'"
citations.urdu: عبداللہ بن مسعود رضی اللہ عنہ سے روایت ہے کہ رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے فرمایا: اللہ تعالیٰ کے کچھ فرشتے زمین میں سیر کرتے پھرتے ہیں جو میری امت کا سلام مجھ تک پہنچاتے ہیں۔
citations.romanUrdu: Abdullah bin Masood RA se riwayat hai keh Rasoolullah SAW ne farmaya: Allah Ta'ala ke kuch farishtay zameen mein ghoomtay phirte hain jo meri ummat ka salam mujh tak pahunchatay hain.
citations.grading: Sahih (graded Sahih; per Darussalam edition, also authenticated by scholars such as Albani, Abu Ghuddah, and Ali Za'i)
citations.relevance: Confirms that a pilgrim's salam upon the Prophet ﷺ — whether said in person at Madinah or from anywhere else in the world — reaches him through Allah's appointed angels, reassuring those unable to travel to Madinah.

citations.arabic: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتَهُۥ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى ٱلنَّبِىِّ ۚ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ صَلُّوا۟ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا۟ تَسْلِيمًا
citations.english: Indeed, Allah confers blessing upon the Prophet, and His angels [ask Him to do so]. O you who have believed, ask [Allah to confer] blessing upon him and ask [Allah to grant him] peace.
citations.urdu: بیشک اللہ اور اس کے فرشتے نبی پر درود بھیجتے ہیں۔ اے ایمان والو! تم بھی ان پر درود اور خوب سلام بھیجا کرو۔
citations.romanUrdu: Beshak Allah aur uske farishtay Nabi (SAW) par durood bhejtay hain. Aey imaan walo! Tum bhi un par durood aur khoob salam bheja karo.
citations.relevance: The Quranic basis for the command to send salawat and salam upon the Prophet ﷺ, the central act performed by pilgrims when visiting his grave in Madinah, though also performable from anywhere.

citations.arabic: لَا تَقُمْ فِيهِ أَبَدًا ۚ لَّمَسْجِدٌ أُسِّسَ عَلَى ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ مِنْ أَوَّلِ يَوْمٍ أَحَقُّ أَن تَقُومَ فِيهِ ۚ فِيهِ رِجَالٌ يُحِبُّونَ أَن يَتَطَهَّرُوا۟ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُطَّهِّرِينَ
citations.english: Do not stand [for prayer] within it - ever. A mosque founded on righteousness from the first day is more worthy for you to stand in. Within it are men who love to purify themselves; and Allah loves those who purify themselves.
citations.urdu: تم اس میں کبھی کھڑے نہ ہونا۔ وہ مسجد جس کی بنیاد پہلے ہی دن سے تقویٰ پر رکھی گئی، اس کے زیادہ لائق ہے کہ تم اس میں کھڑے ہو۔ اس میں ایسے لوگ ہیں جو خوب پاک صاف رہنا پسند کرتے ہیں، اور اللہ پاک صاف رہنے والوں کو پسند کرتا ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Tum us mein kabhi khare mat hona. Jo masjid pehle hi din se taqwa par qaim ki gayi, wo is baat ki zyada haqdar hai keh tum us mein khare ho. Us mein aisay log hain jo khoob paak saaf rehna pasand karte hain, aur Allah paak saaf rehnay walon ko pasand karta hai.
citations.relevance: Classically understood by mufassireen (e.g. Ibn Kathir) primarily as referring to Masjid Quba, with a secondary view (supported by a hadith in Sahih Muslim) that it refers to Masjid an-Nabawi itself; both readings place the verse within the Madinah context, underscoring the special sanctity and virtue of the mosques of Madinah founded on taqwa from their very first day.

rulingSummary: A woman in haiz (menstruation) performs all the rites of Hajj/Umrah as usual — entering ihram, standing at Arafat, staying in Mina, making du'a, etc. — except she may not perform Tawaf until she becomes pure (ghusl after bleeding stops); if her period begins before she can perform Umrah's Tawaf, she changes her intention to include Hajj and delays the Tawaf until purity, and if she is still menstruating at the time of departure, Tawaf al-Wida' (the farewell tawaf) is waived for her entirely with no compensation (dam) required.
keyPoints: Haiz is described in the Quran as a state of harm/impurity during which a woman is to keep away from marital relations until she becomes pure (Quran 2:222) — this underlies the fiqh requirement of tahara (purity) for acts like Tawaf.

A menstruating woman performs every Hajj ritual normally (ihram, wuquf at Arafat, Mina, du'a, etc.) except Tawaf of the Ka'bah, which she must delay until she has become pure and performed ghusl (Sahih al-Bukhari 305 — the hadith of 'A'isha at Sarif).

If menstruation begins before a woman performs Umrah's Tawaf (e.g., upon arrival in Makkah), she changes her niyyah to include Hajj (effectively becoming Qarin), continues with the Hajj rites, and leaves the Umrah's Tawaf and Sa'i pending until she is pure (Sahih Muslim 1211a — the hadith of 'A'isha during Hajjat al-Wada').

Tawaf al-Wida' (the farewell tawaf), though normally required as the last act before leaving Makkah, is completely excused for a menstruating woman — she may depart without performing it and without any compensation (Sahih al-Bukhari 1755).

Salah missed due to haiz is never made up afterward, unlike fasts, which highlights that some acts are excused outright while others (like Tawaf) are merely delayed until purity rather than dropped (Sahih Muslim 335a).

citations.arabic: وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْمَحِيضِ ۖ قُلْ هُوَ أَذًى فَٱعْتَزِلُوا۟ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فِى ٱلْمَحِيضِ ۖ وَلَا تَقْرَبُوهُنَّ حَتَّىٰ يَطْهُرْنَ ۖ فَإِذَا تَطَهَّرْنَ فَأْتُوهُنَّ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلتَّوَّٰبِينَ وَيُحِبُّ ٱلْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ
citations.english: And they ask you about menstruation. Say, "It is harm, so keep away from wives during menstruation. And do not approach them until they are pure. And when they have purified themselves, then come to them from where Allah has ordained for you. Indeed, Allah loves those who are constantly repentant and loves those who purify themselves."
citations.urdu: اور لوگ آپ سے حیض کے بارے میں پوچھتے ہیں، فرما دیں: یہ ایک تکلیف دہ حالت ہے، لہٰذا حیض کی حالت میں عورتوں سے (ہمبستری سے) الگ رہو، اور ان کے قریب نہ جاؤ یہاں تک کہ وہ پاک ہو جائیں۔ پھر جب وہ پاک ہو جائیں تو ان کے پاس جاؤ جہاں سے اللہ نے تمہیں حکم دیا ہے۔ بیشک اللہ توبہ کرنے والوں کو پسند کرتا ہے اور پاکیزگی اختیار کرنے والوں کو پسند کرتا ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Log aap se haiz (periods) ke baare mein poochte hain, keh dijiye: yeh ek takleef wali cheez hai, is liye haiz ki halat mein biwiyon se (humbistari se) alag raho, aur un ke qareeb na jao jab tak woh pak na ho jayein. Phir jab woh pak ho jayein to un ke paas jao jahan se Allah ne tumhein hukm diya hai. Beshak Allah tauba karne walon ko pasand karta hai aur pakizgi ikhtiyar karne walon ko pasand karta hai.
citations.relevance: This is the foundational Quranic ayah establishing haiz as a state requiring specific rulings on abstention and purity — it is the doctrinal basis for fiqh rulings regarding purity (tahara) during menstruation, which underlies why acts requiring ritual purity (such as Tawaf, akin to salah) are not valid during haiz.

citations.citation: Sahih al-Bukhari 305 (Book 6, Kitab al-Haid / Book of Menstrual Periods)
citations.arabic: خَرَجْنَا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم لاَ نَذْكُرُ إِلاَّ الْحَجَّ، فَلَمَّا جِئْنَا سَرِفَ طَمِثْتُ، فَدَخَلَ عَلَىَّ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَأَنَا أَبْكِي فَقَالَ «مَا يُبْكِيكِ». قُلْتُ لَوَدِدْتُ وَاللَّهِ أَنِّي لَمْ أَحُجَّ الْعَامَ. قَالَ «لَعَلَّكِ نُفِسْتِ». قُلْتُ نَعَمْ. قَالَ «فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ شَىْءٌ كَتَبَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَى بَنَاتِ آدَمَ، فَافْعَلِي مَا يَفْعَلُ الْحَاجُّ، غَيْرَ أَنْ لاَ تَطُوفِي بِالْبَيْتِ حَتَّى تَطْهُرِي».
citations.english: Narrated 'Aisha: We set out with the Prophet (ﷺ) for Hajj and when we reached Sarif I got my menses. When the Prophet (ﷺ) came to me, I was weeping. He asked, "Why are you weeping?" I said, "I wish if I had not performed Hajj this year." He asked, "May be that you got your menses?" I replied, "Yes." He then said, "This is the thing which Allah has ordained for all the daughters of Adam. So do what all the pilgrims do except that you do not perform the Tawaf round the Ka'ba till you are clean."
citations.urdu: ہم نبی کریم ﷺ کے ساتھ (حج کے لیے) نکلے، ہماری نیت صرف حج کی تھی۔ جب ہم مقامِ سَرِف پر پہنچے تو مجھے حیض آ گیا۔ نبی کریم ﷺ میرے پاس تشریف لائے تو میں رو رہی تھی۔ آپ ﷺ نے پوچھا: "تم کیوں رو رہی ہو؟" میں نے کہا: "اللہ کی قسم! کاش میں اس سال حج نہ کرتی۔" آپ ﷺ نے فرمایا: "شاید تمہیں حیض آ گیا ہے؟" میں نے کہا: "جی ہاں۔" آپ ﷺ نے فرمایا: "یہ ایک ایسی چیز ہے جو اللہ نے تمام بنات آدم (عورتوں) کے لیے مقدر کر رکھی ہے۔ سو تم وہ سب کچھ کرو جو حاجی کرتے ہیں، سوائے اس کے کہ تم بیت اللہ کا طواف نہ کرو جب تک پاک نہ ہو جاؤ۔"
citations.romanUrdu: Hum Nabi Kareem SAW ke sath (Hajj ke liye) nikle, hamari niyyat sirf Hajj ki thi. Jab hum Sarif pahunche to mujhe haiz aa gaya. Nabi Kareem SAW mere paas tashreef laye to main ro rahi thi. Aap SAW ne pucha: "Tum kyun ro rahi ho?" Maine kaha: "Allah ki qasam! Kaash main is saal Hajj na karti." Aap SAW ne farmaya: "Shayad tumhein haiz aa gaya hai?" Maine kaha: "Ji haan." Aap SAW ne farmaya: "Yeh ek aisi cheez hai jo Allah ne tamam bint-e-Adam (aurton) ke liye likh di hai. So tum wo sab kuch karo jo Hajji karte hain, siwaye is ke ke tum Baitullah ka tawaf na karo jab tak paak na ho jao."
citations.grading: Sahih (recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari, Book of Menstrual Periods, Book 6, Hadith 305)
citations.relevance: Directly answers whether a menstruating (haiz) woman can go about Hajj activities normally: she performs every ritual (staying near Haram, Arafat, Mina, du'a, etc.) except Tawaf, which is delayed until she is pure (ghusl after bleeding stops). This is a primary daleel for the requirement of purity specifically for Tawaf.

citations.arabic: أُمِرَ النَّاسُ أَنْ يَكُونَ آخِرُ عَهْدِهِمْ بِالْبَيْتِ، إِلاَّ أَنَّهُ خُفِّفَ عَنِ الْحَائِضِ
citations.english: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The people were ordered to perform the Tawaf of the Ka'ba (Tawaf-al-Wada') as the last thing before leaving (Mecca), except the menstruating women who were excused.
citations.urdu: حضرت ابن عباس رضی اللہ عنہما سے روایت ہے کہ لوگوں کو حکم دیا گیا کہ (مکہ سے) روانہ ہونے سے پہلے آخری کام بیت اللہ کا طواف (طوافِ وداع) ہو، سوائے حائضہ عورت کے کہ اس کے لیے یہ حکم ہلکا کر دیا گیا (یعنی معاف کر دیا گیا)۔
citations.romanUrdu: Hazrat Ibn Abbas RA se riwayat hai ke logon ko hukum diya gaya ke (Makkah se) rawana hone se pehle aakhri kaam Baitullah ka tawaf (Tawaf-e-Wida) ho, siwaye haiz wali aurat ke ke uske liye yeh hukum halka kar diya gaya (yani maaf kar diya gaya).
citations.grading: Sahih (Bukhari, Book of Hajj, In-book reference: Book 25, Hadith 233; also narrated in Sahih Muslim)
citations.relevance: Establishes that Tawaf-e-Wida (the farewell tawaf, normally Wajib) is completely waived for a menstruating woman with no dam (compensation) required — directly relevant to pilgrims worried about departing while on their period after completing the essential Hajj/Umrah rites.

citations.citation: Sahih Muslim 1211a (Book 15, Hadith 119; USC-MSA Book 7, Hadith 2764)
citations.arabic: خَرَجْنَا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم عَامَ حَجَّةِ الْوَدَاعِ فَأَهْلَلْنَا بِعُمْرَةٍ ثُمَّ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم «مَنْ كَانَ مَعَهُ هَدْىٌ فَلْيُهِلَّ بِالْحَجِّ مَعَ الْعُمْرَةِ ثُمَّ لاَ يَحِلُّ حَتَّى يَحِلَّ مِنْهُمَا جَمِيعًا». قَالَتْ فَقَدِمْتُ مَكَّةَ وَأَنَا حَائِضٌ لَمْ أَطُفْ بِالْبَيْتِ وَلاَ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ فَشَكَوْتُ ذَلِكَ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم فَقَالَ «انْقُضِي رَأْسَكِ وَامْتَشِطِي وَأَهِلِّي بِالْحَجِّ وَدَعِي الْعُمْرَةَ».
citations.english: 'A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) said: We went with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) during the year of the Farewell Pilgrimage. We entered into the state of Ihram for Umra... She said: When I came to Mecca, I was having menses, I neither circumambulated the House, nor ran between as-Safa and al-Marwa. I complained about it to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and he said: Undo your hair, comb it, and pronounce Talbiya for Hajj, and give up Umra (for the time being), which I did.
citations.urdu: حضرت عائشہ رضی اللہ عنہا فرماتی ہیں: ہم رسول اللہ ﷺ کے ساتھ حجۃ الوداع کے سال نکلے، ہم نے عمرے کا احرام باندھا... جب میں مکہ پہنچی تو مجھے حیض آ چکا تھا، نہ میں نے بیت اللہ کا طواف کیا نہ صفا و مروہ کی سعی کی۔ میں نے یہ بات رسول اللہ ﷺ سے بیان کی تو آپ ﷺ نے فرمایا: "اپنے سر کے بال کھول لو، کنگھی کر لو، اور حج کی نیت سے تلبیہ پکارو اور عمرہ چھوڑ دو۔"
citations.romanUrdu: Hazrat Ayesha RA farmati hain: Hum Rasulullah SAW ke sath Hajjatul Wida ke saal nikle, humne Umrah ka ihram bandha... Jab main Makkah pahunchi to mujhe haiz aa chuka tha, na maine Baitullah ka tawaf kiya na Safa-o-Marwah ki sa'i. Maine yeh baat Rasulullah SAW se bayan ki to Aap SAW ne farmaya: "Apne sar ke baal khol lo, kanghi kar lo, aur Hajj ki niyyat se talbiya pukaro aur Umrah chhod do."
citations.grading: Sahih (Sahih Muslim, Book 15 - Kitab al-Hajj, Hadith 119)
citations.relevance: Directly addresses what to do if haiz begins before Umrah tawaf can be performed (before/at arrival in Mecca) — the woman changes her niyyah to include Hajj (converting to Qiran) rather than abandoning the journey, delaying Umrah's tawaf until purification, showing haiz does not stop entry into ihram or the pilgrimage.

citations.grading: Sahih (Muslim, Book 3 - Kitab al-Hayd; in-book reference Book 3, Hadith 83)
citations.arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الرَّبِيعِ الزَّهْرَانِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادٌ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلاَبَةَ، عَنْ مُعَاذَةَ، أَنَّ امْرَأَةً، سَأَلَتْ عَائِشَةَ فَقَالَتْ أَتَقْضِي إِحْدَانَا الصَّلاَةَ أَيَّامَ مَحِيضِهَا فَقَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ أَحَرُورِيَّةٌ أَنْتِ قَدْ كَانَتْ إِحْدَانَا تَحِيضُ عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم ثُمَّ لاَ تُؤْمَرُ بِقَضَاءٍ
citations.english: Mu'adha reported: A woman asked 'A'isha: Should one amongst us complete prayers abandoned during the period of menses? 'A'isha said: Are you a Haruriya? When any one of us during the time of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was in her menses (and abandoned prayer) she was not required to complete them.
citations.urdu: معاذہ رحمہا اللہ بیان کرتی ہیں کہ ایک عورت نے حضرت عائشہ رضی اللہ عنہا سے پوچھا: کیا ہم میں سے کوئی حیض کے دنوں کی چھوٹی ہوئی نمازیں بعد میں قضا کرے؟ حضرت عائشہ رضی اللہ عنہا نے فرمایا: کیا تم حروریہ ہو؟ ہم میں سے جب کوئی رسول اللہ ﷺ کے زمانے میں حائضہ ہوتی تھی تو اسے نماز کی قضا کا حکم نہیں دیا جاتا تھا۔
citations.romanUrdu: Muadha rehmatullah alaiha bayan karti hain ke ek aurat ne Hazrat Ayesha RA se poocha: Kya hum mein se koi haiz ke dinon ki chhooti hui namazein baad mein qaza kare? Hazrat Ayesha RA ne farmaya: Kya tum Harooriya ho? Hum mein se jab koi Rasulullah SAW ke zamane mein haiza hoti thi to usay namaz ki qaza ka hukum nahi diya jata tha.
citations.relevance: Clarifies that Salah missed during haiz is never made up (unlike Sawm/fasting, which is made up later) — relevant background so pilgrims understand which acts are excused outright versus merely delayed, helping explain why Tawaf (unlike Salah) must eventually be made up rather than simply dropped.

rulingSummary: Sa'ee — walking/striding seven times between Safa and Marwah — is an established rite of Hajj and Umrah, following the Prophet's ﷺ own practice, and its performance is binding rather than merely optional despite the outwardly permissive wording of the Qur'an.
keyPoints: Safa and Marwah are named in the Qur'an as being among the symbols (sha'a'ir) of Allah, and walking between them during Hajj or Umrah is sanctioned worship (Quran 2:158).

Although the ayah's wording ('there is no blame on him') sounds permissive, Aisha (RA) clarified that the Prophet ﷺ established Sa'i as a binding practice — 'no one is allowed to omit the Tawaf (Sa'i) between them' (Sahih al-Bukhari 1643).

Sa'ee is performed after Tawaf, beginning at Safa in imitation of the Prophet ﷺ, who said, 'I begin with what Allah began with,' before ascending Safa, facing the Qiblah, and making dhikr and takbir (Sahih Muslim 1218a).
The Prophet ﷺ made du'a and dhikr at Safa and Marwah rather than reciting one single fixed, scripted supplication, repeating similar acts of remembrance at both hills (Sahih Muslim 1218a).
Brisk walking (sa'i/running) is confined only to the low part of the valley (batn al-wadi) between the two hills; the rest of the distance is walked normally (Sahih Muslim 1218a).
After completing Sa'ee, women end their Ihram by trimming a small portion of their hair (taqsir); unlike men, shaving is not obligatory or even permissible for them (Sunan Abi Dawud 1984).

citations.arabic: إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِنْ شَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ ۖ فَمَنْ حَجَّ الْبَيْتَ أَوِ اعْتَمَرَ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَطَّوَّفَ بِهِمَا ۚ وَمَنْ تَطَوَّعَ خَيْرًا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ شَاكِرٌ عَلِيمٌ
citations.english: Indeed, ˹the hills of˺ Ṣafa and Marwah are among the symbols of Allah. So whoever makes the major or minor pilgrimage to the ˹Sacred˺ House, let them walk between ˹the two hills˺. And whoever does good willingly, Allah is truly Appreciative, All-Knowing.
citations.urdu: بے شک صفا اور مروہ اللہ کی نشانیوں میں سے ہیں، پس جو شخص بیت اللہ کا حج یا عمرہ کرے تو اس پر کوئی گناہ نہیں کہ وہ ان دونوں کے درمیان سعی (چکر) کرے، اور جو کوئی از خود نیکی کرے تو بے شک اللہ قدردان، خوب جاننے والا ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Beshak Safa aur Marwa Allah ki nishaniyon mein se hain, to jo shakhs Baitullah ka Hajj ya Umrah kare, us par koi gunah nahi ke wo un dono ke darmiyan sa'ee kare, aur jo koi apni khushi se nekii kare to Allah qadardaan hai, khoob jaanne wala hai.
citations.relevance: This is the foundational Quranic text establishing Safa and Marwah as sacred symbols and that walking (sa'ee) between them in Hajj or Umrah is a legislated, sanctioned rite — the basis for the entire ruling on Sa'ee.

citations.arabic: سَأَلْتُ عَائِشَةَ ـ رضى الله عنها ـ فَقُلْتُ لَهَا أَرَأَيْتِ قَوْلَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى {إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِنْ شَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ فَمَنْ حَجَّ الْبَيْتَ أَوِ اعْتَمَرَ فَلاَ جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَطَّوَّفَ بِهِمَا}... فَقَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ بِئْسَ مَا قُلْتَ يَا ابْنَ أُخْتِي، ثُمَّ قَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ ـ رضى الله عنها ـ وَقَدْ سَنَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم الطَّوَافَ بَيْنَهُمَا، فَلَيْسَ لأَحَدٍ أَنْ يَتْرُكَ الطَّوَافَ بَيْنَهُمَا
citations.english: Narrated 'Urwa: I asked 'Aisha: "How do you interpret the statement of Allah: 'Verily! As-Safa and Al-Marwa are among the symbols of Allah, and whoever performs the Hajj to the Ka'ba or performs 'Umra, it is not harmful for him to perform Tawaf (Sa'i) between them...'" [Urwa initially reasoned that this made Sa'i optional, and 'Aisha rebuked him, saying:] "What a bad thing you have said, O son of my sister! Surely, Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) set the tradition of Tawaf (Sa'i) between Safa and Marwa, so nobody is allowed to omit the Tawaf (Sa'i) between them."
citations.urdu: عروہ نے کہا: میں نے عائشہ رضی اللہ عنہا سے پوچھا کہ اللہ تعالیٰ کے اس فرمان کی کیا تفسیر ہے: بے شک صفا اور مروہ اللہ کی نشانیوں میں سے ہیں، پس جو شخص بیت اللہ کا حج یا عمرہ کرے اس پر ان کے درمیان سعی کرنے میں کوئی حرج نہیں... عائشہ رضی اللہ عنہا نے (عروہ کی غلط تاویل پر) فرمایا: بھانجے! تم نے بری بات کہی۔ رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے صفا و مروہ کے درمیان سعی کو مسنون (لازمی طریقہ) قرار دیا ہے، پس کسی کے لیے جائز نہیں کہ وہ اس سعی کو چھوڑ دے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Urwa ne kaha: Maine Ayesha (رضی اللہ عنہا) se pucha ke Allah ke is farmaan ki kya tafseer hai: 'Beshak Safa aur Marwa Allah ki nishaniyon mein se hain, jo shakhs Baitullah ka Hajj ya Umrah kare us par un ke darmiyan sa'ee karne mein koi harj nahi'... Ayesha (رضی اللہ عنہا) ne (Urwa ki ghalat tawil par) farmaya: Bhanje! tum ne buri baat kahi. Rasulullah ﷺ ne Safa Marwa ke darmiyan sa'ee ko sunnat (lazmi tareeqa) qarar diya hai, kisi ke liye jaiz nahi ke wo ye sa'ee chhod de.
citations.grading: Sahih (authentic) - collected in Sahih al-Bukhari, Book of Hajj, Chapter: Tawaf (Sa'i) between As-Safa and Al-Marwa
citations.relevance: Aisha (RA) clarifies that despite the permissive wording of the ayah (Quran 2:158), which her nephew 'Urwa initially misread as making Sa'i optional, the Prophet ﷺ established Sa'i as a binding practice ('sanna' - set as a sunnah/tradition) — 'no one is allowed to omit it' — which is the direct classical basis for treating Sa'i as an obligatory rite of Hajj/Umrah rather than merely optional.

citations.arabic: ثُمَّ خَرَجَ مِنَ الْبَابِ إِلَى الصَّفَا فَلَمَّا دَنَا مِنَ الصَّفَا قَرَأَ {إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِنْ شَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ} أَبْدَأُ بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ. فَبَدَأَ بِالصَّفَا فَرَقِيَ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى رَأَى الْبَيْتَ فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ فَوَحَّدَ اللَّهَ وَكَبَّرَهُ... ثُمَّ نَزَلَ إِلَى الْمَرْوَةِ حَتَّى إِذَا انْصَبَّتْ قَدَمَاهُ فِي بَطْنِ الْوَادِي سَعَى حَتَّى إِذَا صَعِدَتَا مَشَى حَتَّى أَتَى الْمَرْوَةَ فَفَعَلَ عَلَى الْمَرْوَةِ كَمَا فَعَلَ عَلَى الصَّفَا
citations.english: He then went out of the gate to al-Safa' and as he reached near it he recited: 'Al-Safa' and al-Marwa are among the signs appointed by Allah,' (adding:) I begin with what Allah has commanded me to begin. He first mounted al-Safa' till he saw the House, and facing Qibla he declared the Oneness of Allah and glorified Him... He then descended and walked towards al-Marwa, and when his feet came down in the bottom of the valley, he ran (sa'a), and when he began to ascend he walked till he reached al-Marwa. There he did as he had done at al-Safa'.
citations.urdu: پھر آپ ﷺ دروازے سے نکل کر صفا کی طرف گئے، جب صفا کے قریب پہنچے تو یہ آیت پڑھی: 'بے شک صفا اور مروہ اللہ کی نشانیوں میں سے ہیں'، اور فرمایا: 'میں اس چیز سے شروع کرتا ہوں جس سے اللہ نے شروع کیا'۔ پھر صفا سے شروع کیا، اس پر چڑھے یہاں تک کہ بیت اللہ کو دیکھا، قبلہ رخ ہو کر اللہ کی توحید اور تکبیر بیان کی... پھر مروہ کی طرف اترے، جب وادی کے نشیب میں قدم پڑے تو دوڑے (سعی کی)، اور جب چڑھائی شروع ہوئی تو پیدل چلے یہاں تک کہ مروہ پہنچے، اور مروہ پر بھی وہی کیا جو صفا پر کیا تھا۔
citations.romanUrdu: Phir Aap ﷺ darwaze se nikal kar Safa ki taraf gaye, jab Safa ke qareeb pahunche to ye ayat parhi: 'Beshak Safa aur Marwa Allah ki nishaniyon mein se hain', aur farmaya: 'Main usi cheez se shuru karta hoon jis se Allah ne shuru kiya'. Phir Safa se shuru kiya, us par charhe yahan tak ke Baitullah ko dekha, Qibla rukh ho kar Allah ki tauheed aur takbeer bayan ki... phir Marwa ki taraf utray, jab wadi ke neeche qadam pahunche to daure (sa'ee ki), aur jab charhai shuru hui to paidal chale yahan tak ke Marwa pahunche, aur Marwa par bhi wahi kiya jo Safa par kiya tha.
citations.relevance: This is the detailed hadith of Jabir (RA) describing the Prophet's ﷺ Hajj, establishing: the sequence of Tawaf before Sa'ee, starting at Safa ('I begin with what Allah began with'), the varying dhikr/dua pattern (not one fixed scripted dua) recited at each hill, and the brisk walking/running (sa'a) confined only to the low part of the valley (batn al-wadi) between Safa and Marwah, with normal walking elsewhere.

citations.arabic: لَيْسَ عَلَى النِّسَاءِ حَلْقٌ إِنَّمَا عَلَى النِّسَاءِ التَّقْصِيرُ
citations.english: Narrated Abdullah ibn Abbas: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: Shaving is not a duty laid on women; only clipping the hair is incumbent on them.
citations.urdu: عبداللہ بن عباس رضی اللہ عنہما سے روایت ہے کہ نبی کریم ﷺ نے فرمایا: عورتوں پر سر منڈوانا نہیں ہے، عورتوں پر تو صرف بال کترنا (تھوڑا سا کاٹنا) ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Abdullah bin Abbas (رضی اللہ عنہما) se riwayat hai ke Nabi Kareem ﷺ ne farmaya: Auraton par sar mundwana nahi hai, auraton par sirf bal katarna (thoda sa kaatna) hai.
citations.grading: Sahih li ghairih (graded authentic due to corroborating chains, per Shaykh al-Albani)
citations.relevance: Directly answers whether women can cut their own hair after Sa'ee: women are only required to trim a small amount from their hair (taqsir), not shave, and this trimming is what women themselves do to end Ihram after completing Sa'ee.

rulingSummary: Qasr is the Sunnah practice of shortening the four-rak'ah Fard prayers (Zuhr, Asr, Isha) to two rak'ah while traveling, including throughout the period of Hajj/Umrah travel, as established by the Qur'an and the consistent practice of the Prophet ﷺ and the rightly-guided Khulafa.
keyPoints: Qasr (shortening the 4-rak'ah Fard prayers to 2 rak'ah) is permitted for a traveler, as established in Quran 4:101.
The Prophet ◊ practiced Qasr throughout his journeys, including during his stay of ten days in Makkah for Hajj, as narrated by Anas (RA) in Sahih al-Bukhari 1081.

Ibn 'Umar (RA) testified that the Prophet ◊ never offered more than two rak'ah during travel, and that Abu Bakr, 'Umar, and 'Uthman (RA) followed the same practice (Sahih al-Bukhari 1102) — showing continuity of the practice among the Khulafa Rashidun.

It is also Sunnah, following the Prophet's ◊ example, to leave the optional (Sunnah mu'akkadah) prayers before and after the Fard during travel, as reported from Ibn 'Umar (RA) in Sahih al-Bukhari 1101, citing Quran 33:21 as the basis for following the Prophet's ◊ example.

citations.arabic: وَإِذَا ضَرَبْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَقْصُرُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ إِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَن يَفْتِنَكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلْكَافِرِينَ كَانُوا۟ لَكُمْ عَدُوًّا مُّبِينًا
citations.english: And when you travel throughout the land, there is no blame upon you for shortening the prayer, [especially] if you fear that those who disbelieve may disrupt [or attack] you. Indeed, the disbelievers are ever to you a clear enemy.
citations.urdu: اور جب تم زمین میں سفر کرو تو تم پر کوئی گناہ نہیں کہ نماز میں سے کچھ کم کر دو، اگر تمہیں خوف ہو کہ کافر تمہیں ستائیں گے۔ بے شک کافر تمہارے کھلے دشمن ہیں۔
citations.romanUrdu: Jab tum zameen mein safar karo to tum par koi gunah nahi ke namaz thodi kam kar do (qasr karo), khaas kar agar tumhein khauf ho ke kafir tumhein takleef dein. Kafir tumhare khule dushman hain.
citations.relevance: This is the foundational ayah establishing the permission/basis for Qasr (shortening the 4-rak'ah Fard prayers) for a traveler. While the verse's wording conditions the concession on fear of the disbelievers, scholarly consensus (based on the Prophet's ﷺ consistent practice, e.g., the hadith of Ya'la ibn Umayyah in Sahih Muslim) holds that shortening prayer applies generally during travel, not only when fearing attack.

citations.arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مَعْمَرٍ، قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَارِثِ، قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَنَسًا، يَقُولُ خَرَجْنَا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم مِنَ الْمَدِينَةِ إِلَى مَكَّةَ، فَكَانَ يُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ حَتَّى رَجَعْنَا إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ‏.‏ قُلْتُ أَقَمْتُمْ بِمَكَّةَ شَيْئًا قَالَ أَقَمْنَا بِهَا عَشْرًا‏.‏
citations.english: I heard Anas saying, 'We traveled with the Prophet (ﷺ) from Medina to Mecca and offered two rak'at (for every prayer) till we returned to Medina.' I said, 'Did you stay for a while in Mecca?' He replied, 'We stayed in Mecca for ten days.'
citations.urdu: میں نے انس رضی اللہ عنہ سے سنا، وہ فرماتے ہیں: ہم نبی کریم ﷺ کے ساتھ مدینہ سے مکہ کی طرف نکلے تو آپ ﷺ ہر نماز دو دو رکعت پڑھتے رہے یہاں تک کہ ہم مدینہ واپس آ گئے۔ میں نے پوچھا: کیا آپ لوگ مکہ میں کچھ عرصہ ٹھہرے تھے؟ فرمایا: ہم مکہ میں دس دن ٹھہرے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Anas (RA) farmate hain: hum Nabi Kareem ﷺ ke saath Madina se Makkah gaye to Aap ﷺ har namaz mein do do rakat padhte rahe yahan tak ke hum Madina wapas aa gaye. Poocha gaya: kya aap log Makkah mein kuch dinon thehray thay? Farmaya: hum Makkah mein das din thehray.
citations.relevance: Direct proof that the Prophet ﷺ shortened the 4-rak'ah Fard to 2 rak'ah throughout a journey (including the days spent in Makkah for Hajj/Umrah), establishing the practice of Qasr as the Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ himself for pilgrims.

citations.citation: Sahih al-Bukhari 1102, Book 18 (Shortening the Prayers - At-Taqseer), Hadith 22
citations.arabic: صَحِبْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم فَكَانَ لاَ يَزِيدُ فِي السَّفَرِ عَلَى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، وَأَبَا بَكْرٍ وَعُمَرَ وَعُثْمَانَ كَذَلِكَ
citations.english: Narrated Ibn 'Umar: I accompanied Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and he never offered more than two rak'at during the journey. Abu Bakr, 'Umar and 'Uthman used to do the same.
citations.urdu: میں نے رسول اللہ ﷺ کی صحبت اختیار کی، آپ ﷺ سفر میں دو رکعت سے زیادہ نہیں پڑھتے تھے، اور ابوبکر، عمر اور عثمان رضی اللہ عنہم بھی اسی طرح کرتے تھے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Ibn Umar (RA) farmate hain: main ne Rasoolullah ﷺ ki sohbat ikhtiyar ki, Aap ﷺ safar mein do rakat se zyada namaz nahi padhte thay, aur Abu Bakr, Umar aur Usman (RA) bhi isi tarah karte thay.
citations.relevance: Establishes that the Fard prayer in Qasr stays fixed at 2 rak'ah, consistently practiced by the Prophet ﷺ and the first three Khulafa (Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman), underscoring the strength/near-obligatory weight of Qasr in travel.

citations.citation: Sahih al-Bukhari 1101, Book 18 (Shortening the Prayers - At-Taqseer), Hadith 21 — Chapter: باب من لم يتطوع في السفر دبر الصلاة وقبلها
citations.arabic: بَابُ مَنْ لَمْ يَتَطَوَّعْ فِي السَّفَرِ دُبُرَ الصَّلاَةِ وَقَبْلَهَا. عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ عَاصِمٍ: سَافَرَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ فَقَالَ: صَحِبْتُ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ فَلَمْ أَرَهُ يُسَبِّحُ فِي السَّفَرِ، وَقَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: {لَقَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ فِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ}
citations.english: Chapter: Whoever did not offer optional (Nawafil) prayers before or after the (compulsory) prayer during a journey. Narrated Hafs bin 'Asim: Ibn 'Umar went on a journey and said, "I accompanied the Prophet (ﷺ) and he did not offer optional prayers during the journey, and Allah, the Exalted, says: 'Indeed in the Messenger of Allah you have a good example to follow.'" (Quran 33:21)
citations.urdu: باب: جو شخص سفر میں فرض نماز سے پہلے اور بعد نفل نہ پڑھے۔ حفص بن عاصم سے روایت ہے، ابن عمر رضی اللہ عنہما نے سفر کیا اور فرمایا: میں نے نبی کریم ﷺ کی صحبت اختیار کی، میں نے آپ ﷺ کو سفر میں نفل (سنتِ مؤکدہ) نمازیں ادا کرتے نہیں دیکھا، اور اللہ تعالیٰ فرماتا ہے: 'بیشک تمہارے لیے رسول اللہ ﷺ کی ذات میں بہترین نمونہ ہے۔'
citations.romanUrdu: Bab: Jo shakhs safar mein farz namaz se pehle aur baad nafl na parhe. Hafs bin Asim se riwayat hai, Ibn Umar (RA) ne safar kiya aur farmaya: main ne Nabi Kareem SAW ki sohbat ikhtiyar ki, main ne Aap SAW ko safar mein nafl (sunnat-e-mu'akkadah) namazein ada karte nahi dekha, aur Allah Ta'ala farmata hai: 'Beshak tumhare liye Rasool Allah SAW ki zaat mein behtareen namoona hai.'
citations.relevance: Directly establishes the Prophetic practice during travel (safar/Qasr): Ibn 'Umar's testimony that the Prophet ﷺ did not offer optional (Sunnah mu'akkadah) prayers while traveling, cited alongside Qur'an 33:21 as the basis for following his example. This is the primary evidentiary basis scholars use for the ruling that confirmed Sunnah rak'ahs (other than Witr and Fajr Sunnah, which are separately evidenced elsewhere) are commonly left during travel — a practice of concession, not an outright prohibition.

citations.arabic: فِيهِ ءَايَٰتٌۢ بَيِّنَٰتٌ مَّقَامُ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ ۖ وَمَن دَخَلَهُ كَانَ ءَامِنًا ۗ وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ حِجُّ ٱلْبَيْتِ مَنِ ٱسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلًا ۚ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ عَنِ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
citations.english: In it are clear signs and the standing-place of Abraham. Whoever enters it should be safe. Pilgrimage to this House is an obligation by Allah upon whoever is able among the people. And whoever disbelieves, then surely Allah is not in need of any of His creation.
citations.urdu: اس میں کھلی نشانیاں ہیں، مقامِ ابراہیم ہے، اور جو اس میں داخل ہو جائے وہ امن میں ہے۔ اور اللہ کے لیے لوگوں پر اس گھر کا حج فرض ہے، جو بھی اس تک پہنچنے کی استطاعت رکھتا ہو۔ اور جو کفر کرے تو بےشک اللہ تمام جہانوں سے بے نیاز ہے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Is ghar (Kaaba) mein khuli nishaniyan hain, Maqam-e-Ibrahim hai, aur jo koi is mein dakhil ho jaye woh aman mein hai. Aur Allah ke liye logon par is ghar ka Hajj farz hai, jo bhi wahan tak pohanchne ki taqat rakhta ho. Aur jo inkar kare to Allah tamam jahanon se be-niyaz hai.
citations.grading: n/a (Quran - authenticity not graded like hadith)
citations.relevance: This is the foundational ayah establishing Hajj as a religious obligation upon every able Muslim (subject to istita'ah/capability), and is the core Quranic basis for all Hajj-related fiqh discussions.

citations.arabic: وَأَتِمُّوا الْحَجَّ وَالْعُمْرَةَ لِلَّهِ
citations.english: Complete the pilgrimage and minor pilgrimage for Allah.
citations.urdu: اور اللہ کے لیے حج اور عمرہ کو مکمل کرو۔
citations.romanUrdu: Aur Allah ke liye Hajj aur Umrah ko pura karo.
citations.grading: n/a (Quranic text - authenticity not subject to hadith grading)
citations.relevance: Establishes the general spirit and command that Hajj and Umrah, once begun, must be completed sincerely for the sake of Allah, providing the underlying intent behind detailed rulings on rituals like ihram, rami (stoning), etc.

citations.arabic: وَأَذِّن فِى ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلْحَجِّ يَأْتُوكَ رِجَالًۭا وَعَلَىٰ كُلِّ ضَامِرٍۢ يَأْتِينَ مِن كُلِّ فَجٍّ عَمِيقٍۢ
citations.english: Call ˹all˺ people to the pilgrimage. They will come to you on foot and on every lean camel from every distant path.
citations.urdu: اور لوگوں میں حج کا اعلان کر دو، وہ تیرے پاس پیدل اور ہر دبلے اونٹ پر سوار ہو کر آئیں گے، جو دور دراز راستوں سے چل کر آئیں گے۔
citations.romanUrdu: Aur logon mein Hajj ka elaan kar do, woh tumhare pass paidal aur har dubla ount par sawar ho kar aayenge, jo door daraz raston se chal kar aayenge.
citations.relevance: Establishes the divine call and universal spirit of Hajj as a gathering of believers from every corner of the earth, framing the overall context of the pilgrimage that the miscellaneous questions in this bucket relate to.
